title: Dropbox Sign Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Dropbox Sign** (formerly **HelloSign**) is a transactional e-signature platform. This connector — `DropboxSignConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "dropbox_sign"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the REST surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Account | `/account` | Get authenticated account metadata + quotas |
| Signature Requests | `/signature_request/*` | Send, list, get, cancel, remind, download |
| Templates | `/template/*` | List, get, send-with-template |
| Team | `/team` | List team members |
| Unclaimed Drafts | `/unclaimed_draft/list` | List drafts awaiting claim |
| Embedded Signing | `/signature_request/create_embedded` | Generate iframe sign URLs |
| API Apps | `/api_app` | Register an API App (required for embedded signing) |

The connector normalizes signature requests + templates into `NormalizedDocument` with **tenant-scoped ids** (`id = f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces every user operation as a standalone `async def` method (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::DropboxSignHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate a Dropbox Sign API key

1. Sign in to https://app.hellosign.com (the Dropbox Sign console).
2. Open **Settings → API → API Keys**.
3. Click **Create API Key**, label it (e.g. `shielva-prod`), and copy the value — Dropbox Sign shows it only once.
4. (Optional, for embedded signing) **Settings → API → API Apps → Create API App**. Capture the **Client ID** for the embedded `client_id`.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Dropbox Sign**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `api_key` (secret) | API key from step 3 |
| `client_id` (text, optional) | API App Client ID — only for embedded signing |
| `test_mode_default` (boolean) | `true` for dev/test, `false` for production |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://api.hellosign.com/v3` (rarely needed) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 60 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `api_key` is present, then probes `GET /account` to confirm the key is live. A green status means the credential works.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which hits `GET /account` as a lightweight probe. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `OFFLINE + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 403 → `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with a structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Dropbox Sign uses **HTTP Basic** authentication (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) where the API key is the username and the password is empty. No OAuth, no refresh, no expiry — the key stays valid until you revoke it.

### Credential model

```
Authorization: Basic base64(api_key:)
```

Concretely, with the example key `test-api-key-deadbeef`:

```
base64("test-api-key-deadbeef:") = dGVzdC1hcGkta2V5LWRlYWRiZWVmOg==
Authorization: Basic dGVzdC1hcGkta2V5LWRlYWRiZWVmOg==
```

Note the trailing colon — Dropbox Sign requires the empty-password separator even though no password is sent.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `api_key`, persists non-secret config, probes `GET /account`. Fails fast on 401/403. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a `TokenInfo` whose `access_token` is the api_key (surface compatibility — no exchange). |
| `health_check()` | `GET /account` — minimal probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None. The api_key lives in the install config blob. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
Authorization: Basic base64(api_key:)
Accept:        application/json    (or application/pdf / application/zip on download)
User-Agent:    shielva-dropbox-sign-connector/1.0
```

POST bodies use `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` with bracket-indexed nested keys (`signers[0][email_address]=…`). File uploads use `multipart/form-data` with `file[0]`, `file[1]`, … fields.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `api_key` | secret | yes | `self.api_key` | HTTP Basic username |
| `client_id` | text | no | `self.client_id` | Default API App Client ID for embedded signing |
| `test_mode_default` | boolean | no | `self.test_mode_default` (default `true`) | Default `test_mode` for signature requests |
| `base_url` | text | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `https://api.hellosign.com/v3`) | Override for sandbox / proxy |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 60) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["api_key"]
OPTIONAL_CONFIG_KEYS = ["client_id", "test_mode_default", "base_url", "rate_limit_per_min"]

_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_DROPBOX_SIGN_BASE = "https://api.hellosign.com/v3"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `DropboxSignConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::DropboxSignHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `api_key`, persist non-secret config, probe `GET /account`. Returns `HEALTHY + CONNECTED` on success, `OFFLINE + FAILED` on 401/403, `DEGRADED + AUTHENTICATED` on other transient probe failures.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth — returns a TokenInfo whose `access_token` is the configured api_key (surface compatibility).

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
`GET /account`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Pages through `/signature_request/list`, normalizes each entry → `NormalizedDocument` (tenant-scoped id), and ingests. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Account
children.content:
### `async get_account() -> Dict`
`GET /account` — returns the authenticated account payload, including `account_id`, `email_address`, `is_paid_hs`, and quota counters.

children.title: Signature Requests
children.content:
### `async list_signature_requests(page=1, page_size=20, query=None) -> Dict`
`GET /signature_request/list?page={N}&page_size={P}&query={Q}`. The `query` field is a Dropbox Sign DSL — e.g. `status:pending`, `from:owner@example.com`.

### `async get_signature_request(signature_request_id) -> Dict`
`GET /signature_request/{id}`.

### `async send_signature_request(title, subject, message, signers, file_urls=None, files=None, test_mode=None) -> Dict`
`POST /signature_request/send`. `signers` is `[{name, email_address}, ...]`. Provide either `file_urls` (publicly downloadable PDFs) or `files` (list of `(filename, bytes, content_type)` tuples for multipart upload). `test_mode` defaults to the install-time `test_mode_default`.

### `async cancel_signature_request(signature_request_id) -> Dict`
`POST /signature_request/cancel/{id}`.

### `async remind_signature_request(signature_request_id, email_address) -> Dict`
`POST /signature_request/remind/{id}` with `email_address={signer}`.

### `async download_files(signature_request_id, file_type='pdf') -> bytes`
`GET /signature_request/files/{id}?file_type={pdf|zip}`. Returns raw bytes. (`download_signature_request` remains as a back-compat alias.)

children.title: Templates
children.content:
### `async list_templates(page=1, page_size=20) -> Dict`
`GET /template/list`.

### `async get_template(template_id) -> Dict`
`GET /template/{id}`.

### `async send_with_template(template_id, title, subject, message, signers, custom_fields=None, test_mode=None) -> Dict`
`POST /signature_request/send_with_template`. `signers` is `[{role, name, email_address}, ...]` — every signer role defined on the template must be filled.

children.title: Team · Unclaimed Drafts
children.content:
### `async list_team_members(page=1) -> Dict`
`GET /team` — returns `{team: {name, accounts: [...]}}`.

### `async list_unclaimed_drafts() -> Dict`
`GET /unclaimed_draft/list`.

children.title: Embedded · API Apps
children.content:
### `async create_embedded_signature_request(client_id, title, signers, file_urls=None, test_mode=None) -> Dict`
`POST /signature_request/create_embedded`. Requires an API App `client_id`. Response contains a `signing_url` that can be loaded inside the Dropbox Sign embedded iframe.

### `async create_api_app(name, domain, callback_url=None) -> Dict`
`POST /api_app` — register a new API App. Returns `{api_app: {client_id, ...}}`. Use the returned `client_id` for embedded sign requests.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
DropboxSignError                            # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── DropboxSignAuthError                    # 401 / 403
├── DropboxSignBadRequestError              # 400
├── DropboxSignNotFoundError                # 404
├── DropboxSignConflictError                # 409
├── DropboxSignRateLimitError               # 429 — retry_after_s
├── DropboxSignServerError                  # 5xx
└── DropboxSignNetworkError                 # transport (timeout / DNS / reset)
```

Back-compat aliases:
```
DropboxSignNotFound = DropboxSignNotFoundError
```

Dropbox Sign uses a single error envelope:

```json
{"error": {"error_msg": "...", "error_name": "..."}}
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Exponential backoff `0.5s, 1s, 2s`, retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `DropboxSignNetworkError` |

`Retry-After` headers are honoured for 429 responses when present (clamped to a sane minimum).

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches typed exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)   — logged as warning
Network → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `api_key` blank | Generate a new API key in Settings → API → API Keys; paste it into ACP. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, FAILED)` at install | 401 — API key invalid | Verify the key was copied verbatim. Dropbox Sign keys have no prefix; trailing whitespace can break HTTP Basic. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 — API key revoked | Generate a new key in the Dropbox Sign dashboard and re-install. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 — account suspended or key disabled | Check billing status in Dropbox Sign; re-enable or rotate the key. |
| `dropbox_sign.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (Dropbox Sign per-minute cap) | Client retries with backoff. Reduce `rate_limit_per_min` install field or batch fewer concurrent requests. |
| `send_signature_request requires file_urls or files` ValueError | Call missing both inputs | Provide `file_urls=[...]` (publicly accessible PDFs) OR `files=[(name, bytes, content_type), ...]` (multipart upload). |
| 404 on `download_files` | Signature request not yet complete | Files are only downloadable once `is_complete=true`. Poll `get_signature_request` first. |
| Embedded sign URL returns 'Application not found' | Missing or wrong `client_id` | Pass the API App Client ID from Settings → API → API Apps. Falls back to install-time `client_id` if not passed. |
| `signers must be a non-empty list` ValueError | Empty / malformed signers list | Pass `[{name: '...', email_address: '...'}, ...]`. Empty lists, non-dict entries, or missing keys are rejected client-side. |
| Webhook payloads land but signature mismatch | Webhook signing key mismatch | Webhook verification is not yet a connector responsibility — wire it on the gateway side using the Dropbox Sign `event_hash` HMAC scheme. |
